chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.D-2493 of 2022 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on M.A. 12619/2022 2. For orders on M.A. 12620/2022 20.10.2022 Mr. Muhammad Jamil Ahmed advocate for petitioner. Mr. Ahmed Murtaza A. Arab advocate for respondent No.10. Mr. Zaheeruddin Sahito, advocate for SBCA. Mr. Allah Bachayo Soomro, Addl. AG Sindh along with Ali Sher Mukhtiarkar City, Inspector Sohail Sarwar Jamali on behalf of SSP Hyderabad and SIP Sajid Hussain Arain PS City Hyderabad. ----- This is a disposed of matter. Applicant Muhammad Ali, not even a party in this petition, has filed listed applications under Articles 204 & 199(4-A) of the Constitution on the ground that under the garb of order dated 15.09.2022 passed in this petition containing direction to SBCA to demolish illegal construction over Plot No.1574 admeasuring 145.6 Sq. yards situated at Nain Jo Pir Hyderabad, his house constructed over Plot No.1575 is being razed by SBCA. We have heard him and the other side. It appears that the action of demolition of illegal construction allegedly over the plot owned by the applicant is being undertaken independent of order passed by this court. It has been informed that the applicant’s house is ground plus four story without any approved plan. The applicant despite opportunity given has not been able to place on record or show approved plan of his house and is claiming that in the year 2010 under the Amenity Scheme the construction of his house was regularized, but is not even able to show such proof. It is obvious that the applications filed by the applicant are not maintainable even otherwise in the present proceedings, which have already been culminated.

The applicant despite opportunity given has not been able to place on record or show approved plan of his house and is claiming that in the year 2010 under the Amenity Scheme the construction of his house was regularized, but is not even able to show such proof. It is obvious that the applications filed by the applicant are not maintainable even otherwise in the present proceedings, which have already been culminated. These applications are, therefore, dismissed. JUDGE JUDGE Ali Haider
